About Me

Kainoa Shintaku

AI Architect & Software Engineer | Strategic Consulting | BYU Marriott School of Business

LinkedIn GitHub Resume

My Story

I’m a sophomore at Brigham Young University studying Strategic Management with an emphasis in Finance, carrying a 3.82 GPA and a family history that refuses to let me settle.

Two Legacies, One Purpose

My grandmother, Marguerite Jackson, marched with the NAACP in Alabama during Jim Crow and raised a family as a single Black woman while working in mental health—a field that barely acknowledged her patients, let alone her. She spoke openly about what she endured and taught me that faith and opportunity are meant to be seized.

My grandfather, Kenji Shintaku, never spoke about the internment camps. I learned his story differently—standing at Manzanar when I was ten, finding my family’s names in the registry. His silence taught me that resilience doesn’t always announce itself.

Together, their legacies gave me a simple framework: From everyone who has been given much, much will be demanded (Luke 12:48).

Tested Early

When my father lost his job last year, I depleted my savings and took on a second job to keep my family stable—while maintaining my coursework, captaining the BYU Water Polo Club, and organizing a charity tournament that raised over $500. It was the hardest stretch of my life, and also the smallest taste of what my grandparents endured. That perspective kept me moving.

Where I’m Headed

My goal is to graduate from BYU, earn an MBA from Stanford, and build a venture capital firm that backs founders who understand sacrifice – entrepreneurs solving hard problems in physical tech and software who are willing to endure what building something meaningful requires. In the meantime, I’m building products myself so that when I sit across from a founder, I understand what they’re going through.

Beyond the Resume

When I need to quiet the noise, I surf. Fountain Valley, California shaped me, and the ocean remains where I reset—grateful for what was given, focused on what I’m building with it.

Guiding Principles

Two ideas anchor how I approach work and life:

“You can only change two things in your life: your attitude and your effort.”

“The joy we feel has little to do with the circumstances of our lives and everything to do with the focus of our lives.” — President Russell M. Nelson

These are the filter through which I make decisions.

Education

Brigham Young University – Marriott School of Business

Bachelor of Science in Strategic Management and Statistics
Concentration in Finance | Expected April 2028 | Provo, UT

GPA: 3.82/4.00

Scholarship: BYU Multicultural Scholarship

Relevant Coursework: Investment Analysis, Financial Modeling, Data Analytics, Statistical Methods

Professional Experience

Technical Founder

CareBridge Connect
January 2026 – Present | Provo, UT
  • Developed a full-stack healthcare platform improving transparency between skilled nursing facilities and families, delivering key patient well-being indicators in a clear, accessible interface
  • Built an automated CRM of 200+ healthcare facilities across the Western region, generating 50+ warm leads of potential clients
  • Fundraised $1,000+ in grants for initial funding applied toward software development and go-to-market outreach

Investment Analyst

Nucleus Fund
September 2025 – Present | Salt Lake City, UT
  • Performed due diligence on 15+ early-stage startups by conducting market research, industry expert calls, and financial analysis to evaluate investment potential
  • Contributed to the funding of $500k+ in equity across multiple companies, directly supporting portfolio growth and revenue generation
  • Collaborated with founders to assess how funding would accelerate growth; partnered with analysts to deliver in-depth analyses and pitch decks for investment decisions

Commercial Banking Summer Analyst

Genesis Bank
June 2025 – August 2025 | Newport Beach, CA
  • Supported Genesis Bank’s acquisition of a publicly traded bank by assisting with financial due diligence, utilizing CapIQ and FactSet
  • Worked alongside the President/COO to plan for the implementation and execution of a large-scale backfile for acquired loans
  • Collaborated with deal teams on a market expansion strategy, delivering a proposal that identified $30M+ in potential loan and deposit growth

Ambassador

Snagtag
January 2025 – June 2025 | Lehi, UT
  • Represented Snagtag at the Silicon Slopes Startup Expo, connecting with 8 investors and 20+ potential clients
  • Conducted market research on community impact, providing actionable insights to strengthen product offerings
  • Gathered feedback on the new app, facilitating discussions with software development engineers

Leadership & Development

SEO EDGE Participant

SEO Career
January 2025 – June 2025 | New York, NY
  • Selected for competitive program providing coaching on interview, technical, and professional development
  • Completed industry-specific training focused on developing technical skills and institutional knowledge

Activities President

BYU FlexGE Student Council
January 2024 – April 2024 | Provo, UT
  • Privately consulted 25+ students on academic and professional positioning, helping them secure scholarships and admittance to specialty programs

In-Country Humanitarian Director

The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints
December 2021 – January 2024 | Santo Domingo, Dominican Republic
  • Served two years conducting community outreach in Spanish (fluent) across social media, cold calling, door-to-door, and referral channels
  • Coordinated social and educational events with community leaders, positively impacting underprivileged populations

Skills & Expertise

AI Architecture

  • LLM Integration (OpenAI, Grok, Claude)
  • Prompt Engineering & Reasoning Systems
  • AI-Native Workflow Design
  • Agentic Systems & Automation

Engineering

  • Full-Stack Development (Next.js, React, FastAPI)
  • Python, SQL, VBA
  • Database Design (PostgreSQL, Supabase)
  • HIPAA-Compliant Systems Architecture

Strategic Consulting

  • AI Integration Strategy
  • Due Diligence & Market Analysis
  • Financial Modeling & Valuation
  • Go-to-Market & Product Strategy

Organizations

Leadership Roles: President, Trading Desk at BYU | Captain, Water Polo Club

Professional Clubs: AI in Business Association | Management Consulting Association | Venture Capital Club

Other Activities: Student Athlete & Volunteer Coach (Water Polo) | Physical & Mental Health Advocate

Languages

Spanish – Fluent (reading, writing, and speaking)

Gained through two years of full-time service in the Dominican Republic.

What I’m Looking For

I’m focused on roles at the intersection of AI architecture, software engineering, and strategic consulting — building and advising on systems that matter. I want to work where deep technical execution meets real business problems: designing AI-native workflows, architecting enterprise systems, and translating complex technology into strategic advantage.

My financial background isn’t gone — it’s a lens. Understanding how deals are structured, how markets move, and how investors think makes me a sharper engineer and a better strategic partner. I evaluate problems the way a builder and an analyst would simultaneously: not just “does this work?” but “does this compound?”

Let’s Connect

I’m always interested in discussing AI architecture, software engineering, and where strategic consulting meets technical execution.

Download Resume Connect on LinkedIn
